nodes.label: AI for repetitive simple tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI for routine tasks like some light scheduling

nodes.label: AI accuracy in numerical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: The facts provided are usually accurate but often incomplete

nodes.label: AI for exploratory tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Exploratory tasks relating to new ideas, especially early on

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: I am more likely to collaborate with it throughout a task

nodes.label: AI as skill development enabler
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to instead see it as an opportunity to enhance those skills I already have

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: I was able to schedule a week's worth of meetings in about ten minutes

nodes.label: Trustworthiness and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: AI models have unfortunately been inaccurate about references to academic articles

nodes.label: Creative autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: it delays finding connections or new ideas

nodes.label: Skill development and learning
nodes.grounding_span: try to be able to do more

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for scheduling
nodes.grounding_span: I found that very beneficial and a great relief

nodes.label: Skepticism toward AI citation accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: AI models have unfortunately been inaccurate about references to academic articles

nodes.label: Preference for self-directed exploration
nodes.grounding_span: I start out by looking for myself at original sources

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: I hope to transfer more routine tasks to AI and to be able to collaborate more closely

nodes.label: Iterative prompt refinement
nodes.grounding_span: I will often have to refine what it is that I am looking for from the AI

nodes.label: Independent exploration and self-directed learning about AI
nodes.grounding_span: I start out by looking for myself at original sources and then use AI to fill in the blanks
